sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.61 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: The group \({ CT}_{ }( )\) is finitely generated if and only if \( \) is finite. If \( = \), then it is isomorphic to the finitely presented (first) Higman--Thompson group (J.\,P.\,McDermott, see Remark 1.4 in S.\,Kohl, J.~Group Theory, { 20}, no.\,5 (2017), 1025--1030). Is it always finitely presented if \( \) is finite?

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
